[2019-08-12 18:50] ruinedshadows: if you are display rated 1500, then win 10x in a row versus 2200 players, your "secret" rating will shoot up to close to 2200 but you can only gain a max of about 30-40 pts per win,  so you are still way under your "secret" rating by at least 200 points, so if you lose an 11th game to another 2200 player you might still gain +1 or 2 points as the rating system is trying to get your two numbers to converge. at least that's my understanding...I could be completely wrong though
[2019-08-12 18:52] mrguy888: That is not how any rating system I know of works.
[2019-08-12 18:53] ruinedshadows: I thought the display rating was capped for a per game gain/loss
[2019-08-12 18:54] awaclus: Isn't that not a terrible explanation of how TrueSkill works?
[2019-08-12 18:55] awaclus: Well, the point gain isn't capped
[2019-08-12 18:56] ruinedshadows: it's not linear though, so it essentially is capped
[2019-08-12 18:56] mrguy888: As long as you played the 20 or so games in master tier to get rid of the weird behavior that has never been fixed, your display rating in prismata is always 350 points higher than your true rating.
[2019-08-12 18:56] awaclus: But your visible rating will converge with your estimated rating as the system becomes more confident in it
[2019-08-12 18:57] ruinedshadows: that's a third separate number afaik
[2019-08-12 18:58] ruinedshadows: I guess my scenario doesn't work either because a 1500 can't get matched versus a 2200 on ladder anyways
[2019-08-12 18:58] mrguy888: In systems that have multiple variables, you can have an mmr of 2200 or something, but your high uncertainty means your display rating is like 1700 which means the system is 90% sure your rating is higher than 1700. If you lose your rating will drop but your uncertainty will reduce and they may now be 90% sure you are above 1705.
[2019-08-12 18:58] ruinedshadows: but I'm pretty sure on a new account you can get rating gain from a loss by having exactly that type of situation happen
[2019-08-12 19:00] mrguy888: You can get rating from a loss in the first 20 or so games of master tier. That is because when you hit master tier, your display rating goes from 350 above your mmr to something like 100 above your mmr. That is why people lose 100+ points when they win and hit master tier. Over the next 20 games or so it goes back to exactly 350 above your mmr. This is how you can gain rating on a loss with a prismata smurf.

[2019-08-22 01:50] ruinedshadows: I feel like twitch overall is the best platform at its core, it looks nice and works well however, recently twitch has been getting worse/sitting on its laurels while other platforms have launched features that seem nice, they just don't have the critical mass yet. Twitch dashboard is a bit clunky to use imo. Additionally, mixer specifically has better co-streaming options and a couple other features for casters to give a more interactive experience, at least from what I've read. They also have more inclination to entice viewers and even small streamers to come to their platforms so afaik their rewards programs are better. They also have integrated free chat rewards for just simply watching.
